crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_category: Malware Command and Control Activity Detected
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET MALWARE Ransomware/Cerber Checkin 2"; dsize:<11; content:"hi"; depth:2; fast_pattern; pcre:"/^[a-f0-9]{7,}$/R"; threshold: type both, track by_src, count 1, seconds 60; reference:md5,ac4d7fb5739862e9914556ed5d50f84f; classtype:command-and-control; sid:2023453; rev:5; metadata:attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2016_03_28, deployment Perimeter, former_category MALWARE, signature_severity Major, tag Ransomware, updated_at 2016_10_27, mitre_tactic_id TA0040, mitre_tactic_name Impact, mitre_technique_id T1486, mitre_technique_name Data_Encrypted_for_Impact;)

alert udp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 6892 ( msg:"MALWARE-CNC Win.Trojan.Cerber outbound registration attempt"; flow:to_server; dsize:9; content:"hi0",fast_pattern,nocase; pcre:"/hi0[0-9a-f]{6}/"; metadata:impact_flag red; reference:url,www.virustotal.com/en/file/a5ff5f861bbb1ac7c6fd44f303f735fac01273ce2ae43a8acb683076192fcfcc/analysis/1462465221/; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:38885; rev:3; )

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Hidden virtual network computing (HVNC) is a tactic that attempts to take control of a system via specific trojan modules. For example, Gozi has an HVNC module in order to use this kind of attack. Also this attack abuses Windows features such as CreateDesktop and cross-process window subclassing to implement an invisible environment for VNC to run.

values.CommandLine: /d /c taskkill /t /f /im "file.exe" > NUL & ping -n 1 127.0.0.1 > NUL & del "C:\Users\george\Desktop\file.exe" > NUL

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects a method often used by ransomware. Which combines the "ping" to wait a couple of seconds and then "del" to delete the file in question. Its used to hide the file responsible for the initial infection for example

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Creation of an WerFault.exe in Unusual Folder
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects WerFault copoed to a suspicious folder, which could be a sign of WerFault DLL hijacking

values.Description: Command Line Interface for Microsoft\xae Volume Shadow Copy Service

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

values.CommandLine: /d /c taskkill /t /f /im "file.exe" > NUL & ping -n 1 127.0.0.1 > NUL & del "C:\Users\george\Desktop\file.exe" > NUL

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity.
Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how.
Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Creation of an Executable by an Executable
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of an executable by another executable

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Adversaries may stop services or processes in order to conduct Data Destruction or Data Encrypted for Impact on the data stores of services like Exchange and SQL Server.
